metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 Q
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication was made. Employer, breeder, applicant, holder, assignee, editor, licensee, and nursery roles remain distinct. Company-scale and acquisition-relevance observations are screening signals, not financial conclusions. Profiles whose aggregated records span different legal holders, employers, or unrelated crop programs contain record_split_required in webResearchStatus.

profiles.ownershipType: private owner-led fruit-breeding company principal and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Dr. Michael Neumüller, managing director of Bayerisches Obstzentrum GmbH & Co. KG (BayOZ). BayOZ's legal notice and licensing agreement establish his management role, while official cultivar pages identify him as breeder and, for some cultivars, identify BayOZ and/or Neumüller as variety-rights holder. The aggregate also includes work conducted through the Technical University of Munich and earlier public-program relationships, so breeder credit is not converted into BayOZ ownership without cultivar-level title evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Bayerisches Obstzentrum GmbH & Co. KG, Am Süßbach 1, 85399 Hallbergmoos, Germany
profiles.leadershipSummary: Neumüller combines technical leadership, management, and substantial breeding credit. BayOZ has a commercial licensing platform and staff support, but the public material does not identify a named ownership or technical successor, creating a founder-concentration diligence point.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official legal notice and contact page establish Neumüller's current management affiliation, and BayOZ cultivar pages establish the relationship for named recent varieties. Historical TUM or third-party holder records still require separate treatment.

webResearchSources.label: BayOZ legal identity and managing director
BayOZ commercial contact
BayOZ cultivar and breeder evidence

profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority private target. Split BayOZ-owned or BayOZ-managed rights from TUM, legacy-program, personally held, or third-party records before scoring. Verify cap table, title-by-title ownership, licensing economics, trademarks, and succession depth.

profiles.ownershipType: family-owned international stone-fruit breeding, production, and licensing company
profiles.ownershipSummary: PSB Producción Vegetal S.L. is the operating company behind a family-founded stone-fruit breeding platform established by the Buffat family. Its official site describes PSB as breeder, editor, protector, and distributor and reports 130 patented varieties, 75 hectares of breeding and testing, and more than 40,000 new hybrids annually. Registry observations exceed that public count because the dashboard counts jurisdictions, repeat denomination records, and breeder-role observations rather than unique company-owned cultivars.
profiles.headquarters: Pliego, Murcia, Spain, with breeding and testing operations in Spain, France, and international trial locations
profiles.leadershipSummary: Founder Philippe Buffat died in May 2026. PSB appointed Elena Aguarón CEO in March 2026; Stéphane Buffat remains Founder and Chief Technology Officer, Agnès Chapus Buffat leads intellectual property, and a broader technical and commercial team provides institutional continuity. The platform is materially larger than Eden's stated small-target range based on its acreage, production, global reach, and portfolio breadth.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The exact dashboard name is the family company's operating identity; no outside parent or completed acquisition was identified in the reviewed public sources.

webResearchSources.label: PSB history, scale, and family-company status
PSB current leadership and IP contact
PSB breeding, protection, and portfolio count

profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic-scale benchmark or partnership profile, not a likely $1-5 million EBITDA target. Reconcile the dashboard's jurisdiction-level observations to PSB's approximately 130 protected varieties before presenting any cultivar count as a company portfolio count.

profiles.ownershipType: Russian public-university grape scientist and historical multi-institution breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The malformed mixed-case transliteration resolves to Leonid Petrovich Troshin (Леонид Петрович Трошин), a professor of viticulture at Kuban State Agrarian University. His career and the aggregated grape records span Soviet-era and Russian research work, Kuban State Agrarian University, an affiliated small innovation enterprise, and collaborations with other institutes and independent breeders. Named breeder status does not establish personal title or a single university owner across all 128 observations.
profiles.headquarters: Kuban State Agrarian University, Krasnodar, Russian Federation
profiles.leadershipSummary: Troshin led the university's viticulture department from 1996 to 2019 and remained listed as a professor in 2026. He joined the Association of Winegrowers and Winemakers of Russia team in January 2026. The long career and changing institutional affiliations create a succession and record-allocation issue, but not a standalone private acquisition target.
profiles.primaryContactName: Kuban State Agrarian University Department of Viticulture
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official university staff card establishes Troshin's current and long-running academic affiliation, but it does not prove that the university holds every variety right credited to him across earlier institutes and collaborations.

webResearchSources.label: Kuban State Agrarian University staff profile
Kuban State Agrarian University innovation-enterprise relationship
2026 professional status and department-leadership history

profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not score as an individual owner. Split records by denomination, filing year, applicant/holder, address, co-breeder team, and institution. Preserve the exact dashboard spelling as an alias only and display the normalized name Leonid Petrovich Troshin.

profiles.ownershipType: family-owned global blueberry genetics and nursery company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Fall Creek Farm & Nursery, Inc. is a private blueberry genetics, nursery-stock, and grower-support company founded by Dave and Barbara Brazelton in 1978. The company operates its own private breeding program and also commercializes genetics obtained through public and private partnerships. Consequently, the dashboard must distinguish Fall Creek-origin selections from licensed, jointly bred, and third-party genetics.
profiles.headquarters: 39318 Jasper-Lowell Road, Lowell, Oregon 97452, United States, with nursery and R&D operations across the Americas, Europe, South Africa, and China
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Brazelton family repurchased the former partners' shares in 2010 and the second generation holds operating roles. Marc de Beer became CEO in October 2025 and Kevin Murphy is executive chair. Fall Creek reports more than 40 million plants of 100-plus varieties supplied annually across 59 countries, making it a strategic-scale benchmark rather than a small acquisition candidate.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: This is the legal company name used by Fall Creek's current public materials. The parent mapping applies to company-origin records only and does not override cultivar-specific third-party holders or licensors.

webResearchSources.label: Fall Creek company, history, and breeding program
Fall Creek ownership and family succession history
Fall Creek current CEO and operating scale

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exclude from the core small-target ranking. Use as a strategic benchmark and possible licensing or commercial partner. Reconcile Fall Creek-origin, partnership, acquired-rights, and third-party licensed varieties before reporting a legal portfolio count.

profiles.ownershipType: private Japanese fruit-nursery president and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Shigetaka Sakurai (櫻井茂隆), representative director of Nippon Ryokusan Co., Ltd. Japan's MAFF register identifies Sakurai as breeder and Nippon Ryokusan as registered holder for RYOKU NH-12 and related fruit cultivars; Australian PBR material likewise identifies the company as applicant and Sakurai as breeder for RYOKU AP-11. The company, not Sakurai personally, should be treated as holder where those registers say so.
profiles.headquarters: Nippon Ryokusan Co., Ltd., 2534 Imai, Matsumoto, Nagano 390-1131, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Sakurai is both representative director and the named breeder behind a broad apple, blueberry, plum, and pear portfolio. The official company page does not identify a next-generation leader, so management and technical succession require diligence despite the presence of an operating nursery company.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Representative Director, Nippon Ryokusan Co., Ltd.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official company page names Sakurai as representative director, while the MAFF variety register names the company as holder and Sakurai as breeder for the reviewed RYOKU cultivars.

webResearchSources.label: Nippon Ryokusan official company and leadership profile
MAFF RYOKU NH-12 holder and breeder record
Australian PBR journal for RYOKU AP-11

profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential focused private target. Roll source-backed records to Nippon Ryokusan while preserving Sakurai as breeder. Verify company ownership, financial scale, successor depth, foreign agents and licensees, and title for each non-Japanese filing.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as JoAnne F. Coss, a historical Driscoll Strawberry Associates strawberry breeder. Contemporary public records identify her as a Driscoll employee, and U.S. patents and Driscoll litigation materials identify Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. or Driscoll's as assignee and owner of the reviewed cultivars. Coss is an inventor/breeder, not the personal owner. The two apple observations in the aggregate are not supported by the reviewed career and cultivar evidence and require separate identity review.
profiles.headquarters: Historical program: Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Watsonville, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Coss's source-backed breeding activity is historical and team-based. A 2025 public biography describes her Driscoll work in the past tense, supporting former or retired status. Driscoll has a large continuing breeding organization, so there is no independent succession or acquisition case around Coss.
profiles.candidateParent: Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. / Driscoll's, Inc.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Employment and assignee evidence converge for the reviewed strawberry portfolio. The correct Driscoll legal entity and any assignment history must still be preserved record by record.

webResearchSources.label: Contemporary statement of Driscoll employment
Coss patent portfolio and Driscoll assignees
USDA cultivar register and Driscoll breeding teams

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone target and roll only the source-backed strawberry records to the applicable Driscoll entity. Quarantine the apple observations until their underlying names, crop, registration numbers, applicants, and addresses are rechecked.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Enrique Masiá Serrador, breeder of a substantial strawberry portfolio associated with the Masiá Ciscar group. U.S. plant-patent records show Masiá Serrador assigning A13-series cultivars to Masiá Ciscar S.A. Newer records identify Masiá Breeding S.L. as applicant or holder for MC-series selections, while Spanish bulletins show changes among holder and licensee roles. The individual, Masiá Ciscar entities, and Masiá Breeding are distinct and cannot be collapsed without filing-specific assignment evidence.
profiles.headquarters: Masiá group operations, Carretera de La Redondela km 1.2, 21440 Lepe, Huelva, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Masiá Serrador is the concentrated technical breeder behind the reviewed portfolio. The wider family group and dedicated Masiá Breeding entity provide operational continuity, but public sources do not establish the complete cap table, management responsibilities, or whether a named technical successor has been developed.
profiles.candidateParent: Masiá Ciscar / Masiá Breeding, record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: U.S. assignment records support Masiá Ciscar for the reviewed A13 cultivars; newer PBR records support Masiá Breeding for specific MC cultivars. Neither relationship justifies a blanket parent rollup across the full aggregate.

webResearchSources.label: A13-26 inventor, assignment, and Masiá Ciscar assignee
CFIA MC18-20 Masiá Breeding rights record
Masiá Ciscar official group site

profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential private target, but the record split is mandatory. Build a cultivar-by-cultivar timeline across Masiá Ciscar S.A./S.L., Masiá Breeding S.L., the individual breeder, foreign holders, licensees, and trademark owners before scoring enterprise value.

profiles.ownershipType: third-generation family-owned fruit-and-nut nursery with separate breeding IP company
profiles.ownershipSummary: The Burchell Nursery, Inc. is an independent California commercial nursery founded in 1942 and led by the Burchell family. Its official site states that Burchell Breeding Inc. (BBI), created in 2021, owns the group's intellectual property and develops international licenses. The nursery is therefore the operating and propagation company, while BBI is the stated IP owner for the in-house portfolio; third-party varieties sold or licensed by Burchell remain separate.
profiles.headquarters: 12000 State Highway 120, Oakdale, California 95361, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Tom Burchell is the third-generation owner and president, following Irvin and Bill Burchell. The official site reports more than 1,200 acres, over 150,000 square feet of greenhouse space, and more than three million trees sold annually. Family continuity is visible, including a younger generation, but the platform appears materially larger than Eden's target range and the BBI/nursery ownership structure requires diligence.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Burchell's official international-representatives page expressly states that BBI owns the intellectual property. This applies to the in-house portfolio and does not make BBI owner of third-party varieties sold or represented by the nursery.

webResearchSources.label: Burchell ownership, leadership, succession, and scale
Burchell Breeding Inc. program
BBI intellectual-property ownership and international licensees

profiles.webResearchNotes: Private strategic benchmark or potential carve-out/partnership profile, not an obvious small whole-company acquisition. Separate BBI-owned genetics, nursery operations, third-party breeding collaborations, and represented varieties before presenting portfolio counts or assigning records.

profiles.canonicalName: University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture
profiles.ownershipType: public land-grant university agricultural research and licensing institution
profiles.ownershipSummary: The University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture operates the Arkansas Agricultural Experiment Station Fruit Breeding Program and its Technology Commercialization Office licenses university-developed cultivars. The program reports approximately 70 genotypes brought to market across blackberry, blueberry, grape, muscadine, peach, nectarine, and historical strawberry work. The university is the institutional commercialization route, not its individual breeders or licensed propagators.
profiles.headquarters: Division of Agriculture, University of Arkansas System, Fayetteville, Arkansas, United States; Fruit Research Station, Clarksville, Arkansas
profiles.leadershipSummary: Margaret Worthington directs the program, with Carmen Johns as assistant fruit breeder. The program transitioned from founder James N. Moore to John R. Clark and then to Worthington, demonstrating deliberate institutional succession and a durable research bench.
profiles.candidateParent: University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture
profiles.candidateParentBasis: This is already the relevant public institutional owner and licensing organization; individual breeders and licensed nurseries should roll to it only when cultivar records identify university ownership.

webResearchSources.label: Official fruit-breeding program, leadership, count, and licensing contact
Official patented-fruit and Technology Commercialization overview
Official licensed-propagator schedule

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institution; exclude from acquisition ranking. Preserve it as a licensing, partnership, and talent-source profile. Do not mistake licensed propagators for owners or count the same cultivar once per licensee.

profiles.ownershipType: historical and continuing corporate berry breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Thomas M. Sjulin, a long-running Driscoll breeder and former director of strawberry research and production management. Patents and PBR records identify Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. or Driscoll's, Inc. as holder or assignee for the reviewed strawberry and raspberry cultivars. A 2025 Canadian grant still names Sjulin as a breeder of DrisRaspTwentyThree but lists the other breeders as Driscoll employees and Sjulin only by residence, so current employee status is not inferred. The aggregate avocado and nectarine records are not supported by the reviewed evidence and require a separate identity split.
profiles.headquarters: Historical program: Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Watsonville, California; current public records place Sjulin in Aromas, California
profiles.leadershipSummary: Sjulin's career spans decades and includes senior research leadership, but the rights are held within Driscoll's continuing global breeding organization. The recent raspberry grant suggests ongoing inventor participation or legacy credit, not an independent operating company or personal portfolio.
profiles.candidateParent: Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. / Driscoll's, Inc.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Patent and PBR sources consistently name a Driscoll entity as assignee or holder for the source-backed strawberry and raspberry portfolio. Legal-entity changes and unsupported non-berry records must remain separate.

webResearchSources.label: CFIA Kulshan holder and breeder record
USDA record of Driscoll role and research leadership
Sjulin patent portfolio and Driscoll assignees

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone target. Roll only verified Driscoll berry records to the applicable Driscoll legal entity. Quarantine avocado, nectarine, and any other non-berry observations until applicant, address, co-breeder, and registration evidence establishes whether they are the same person.

profiles.ownershipType: deceased nursery entrepreneur and historical consortium apple breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The reversed dashboard name resolves to Francesco Tagliani, founder of Tagliani Vivai and one of the three founding nursery entrepreneurs behind C.I.V. Consorzio Italiano Vivaisti. Canadian PBR and U.S. patent records identify CIV as applicant, holder, or assignee for the reviewed apple portfolio and Tagliani as one member of the breeding team. He is deceased and should not be treated as a current personal owner. The six peach observations are not supported by the reviewed CIV apple evidence and require filing-level allocation.
profiles.headquarters: Historical CIV program: S. Giuseppe di Comacchio, Ferrara, Italy; historical nursery: Argenta/Ostellato, Ferrara, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: CIV commemorates Tagliani as a deceased founder. CIV continues as an institutional breeding and variety-development consortium supported by founding nurseries, and the Tagliani family nursery continues through later generations. This is completed institutional and family succession, not a sole-breeder succession target.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: CIV is the named applicant/holder or assignee for the reviewed apple cultivars and identifies Tagliani's nursery as part of its founding network. This does not allocate unsupported peach records.

webResearchSources.label: CFIA CIV holder and Tagliani breeder record
CIV current research and founding-nursery structure
University of Ferrara memorial record for CIV founders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Normalize display order to Francesco Tagliani. Suppress as an individual target and roll source-backed CIV apples to CIV. Review the peach records separately and do not transfer Tagliani Vivai nursery operations or third-party cultivar rights to CIV without registry evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: small private French fruit-variety IP management and licensing company under safeguard plan
profiles.ownershipSummary: S.A.R.L. Benoit Escande Editions, also branded BEE, is the Escande family's variety-edition and intellectual-property company. Public French company information identifies Benoit Escande as manager and shows the company active under a court-approved safeguard plan from June 2025 following a 2024-2025 safeguard proceeding. Official catalogs show that BEE manages both Escande-origin varieties and varieties originating with third-party breeders, so editor or commercial manager status must not be treated as original breeder or owner status.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Benoit Escande remains manager of a small, concentrated IP entity within a wider family breeding, nursery, production, and distribution group. The active safeguard plan, small disclosed company size, trademark portfolio, and breadth of managed varieties create a potentially actionable special-situation profile, but creditor restrictions, intercompany rights, and financial viability require legal and financial diligence.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Escande materials place BEE alongside breeding, nursery, fruit-production, and distribution entities, but no reviewed source establishes a single legal parent or allows assets of those entities to be consolidated.

webResearchSources.label: BEE official company contact
Current French company status and safeguard plan
Escande group roles and managed-variety scope

profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority special-situation diligence candidate. Split original breeder, legal holder, editor, maintainer, licensee, and trademark owner for every variety. Confirm the safeguard plan, creditor and court constraints, cash generation, related-party agreements, trademark renewals, and which rights are actually transferable.

profiles.ownershipType: retired Belgian family-nursery principal and private apple breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Johan Nicolaï, second-generation operator of N.V. Johan Nicolaï and founder of sister breeding company Zouk. U.S. patents identify Zouk B.V.B.A. as assignee for recent ZOUK selections and Johan as inventor. Earlier cultivars and mutations involve N.V. Johan Nicolaï and, in some cases, separate holders such as Better3fruit. The individual, nursery, breeding company, and commercial partners must remain separate legal roles.
profiles.headquarters: Brustem Industriepark, Lichtenberglaan 2050, B-3800 Sint-Truiden, Belgium
profiles.leadershipSummary: Johan Nicolaï is officially retired but remains involved. Third-generation family members Laura, Vincent, and Michiel Nicolaï now operate the nursery and Zouk, with Laura focused on genetics and cultivar development. The clear family succession and international licensing network reduce sole-founder risk.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official history and U.S. assignment records support Zouk as holder of recent ZOUK-coded cultivars. Historical N.V. Johan Nicolaï, Better3fruit, and partner-held records require separate allocation.

webResearchSources.label: Zouk official family and company history
ZOUK31 inventor and Zouk assignment
Zouk current family contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll recent source-backed ZOUK cultivars to Zouk while preserving Johan as inventor. Split historical nursery, Better3fruit, mutation, and partner records before scoring. The business may be a relationship or partnership target, but current scale, cap table, and willingness to transact remain unverified.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university senior professor and grape breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Enrico Peterlunger, senior professor of viticulture at the University of Udine. Canadian PBR and university technology-transfer materials identify the University of Udine and Istituto di Genomica Applicata as joint applicants or owners of the reviewed disease-resistant grape varieties, with Peterlunger as one member of the breeding team. Commercial nursery licensees do not replace the university and IGA as titleholders.
profiles.headquarters: University of Udine, Via Palladio 8, 33100 Udine, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Peterlunger initiated the disease-resistant grape program in 1998 and now holds senior-professor status. The program has a broad multi-researcher bench, institutional technology-transfer support, and commercial nursery licensees, so it is not dependent on Peterlunger as a private owner.
profiles.primaryContactName: University of Udine Technology Transfer Office
profiles.candidateParent: University of Udine and Istituto di Genomica Applicata
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The CFIA Fleurtai record and university technology-transfer sheet identify joint institutional ownership. Cultivar-level registers control any exception or later assignment.

webResearchSources.label: CFIA Fleurtai joint holders and breeder team
University technology-transfer ownership and licensing sheet
University of Udine current research group and status

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone acquisition target and roll the reviewed varieties to the University of Udine/IGA holder group. Preserve nursery partners as licensees and Peterlunger and collaborators as breeders.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university strawberry breeder and field-program leader
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Glenn S. Cole, strawberry breeder at the University of California, Davis. UC Davis states that inventions and discoveries made by program employees belong to the university, and official release materials identify Cole as breeder and field manager for the UC Davis strawberry portfolio. No reviewed source supports personal ownership by Cole.
profiles.headquarters: University of California, Davis, One Shields Avenue, Davis, California 95616, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Cole joined the UC Davis strawberry program in 2015 after 32 years in the seed industry. Program leadership moved from Steven Knapp to Mitchell Feldmann in 2024, while Cole remained breeder within a staff of more than 20. This is a durable institutional program with documented succession and bench depth.
profiles.candidateParent: The Regents of the University of California
profiles.candidateParentBasis: UC Davis's program backgrounder expressly states university ownership of employee inventions, and the program's official materials identify Cole's employee-breeder role.

webResearchSources.label: UC Davis Glenn Cole official profile
UC Davis ownership and program structure backgrounder
UC Davis current program leadership and bench

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone target and roll source-backed records to The Regents of the University of California. Retain Cole as breeder, preserve cultivar-level co-breeders, and route commercial diligence through UC's plant-variety licensing organization.
